acceptance: A single owner creates, configures, polls, and shuts down the libtorrent session inside aria2-next lifecycle rules.

scope: Alert polling and torrent state transitions
acceptance: Libtorrent alerts update request state, wake the engine, report errors, and avoid busy loops.
notes: Alert handling is intentionally small; richer status mapping continues in BTM-005.

checkpoint: Remove native runtime scheduling from BT path
acceptance: Torrent handles no longer schedule TrackerWatcherCommand, ActivePeerConnectionCommand, DHTGetPeersCommand, PeerChokeCommand, or native peer commands.
verification: `testCreateInitialCommandUsesLibtorrentRuntime` asserts a libtorrent-backed request group schedules exactly one `LibtorrentCommand`.
notes: Physical deletion remains BTM-008 after status, resume, and controls are migrated.
